name: Content, Data & State Patterns
description: Rules for data display, content patterns, loading states, empty states, error handling, and iconography. AI agents: use these rules to correctly render any content scenario.

tables.headerStyle: Uppercase, label typography, secondary text color, wide letter-spacing

sorting.icon: Arrow up/down next to sortable header

tables.emptyState: Center message in table body: 'No data to display' with optional action
tables.responsive: Below md: convert to card layout or horizontal scroll with sticky first column

lists.dividers: Optional. Use 1px border-bottom between items in dense lists.

cards.layout: Use cardGrid layout pattern. Min card width: 280px.

feeds.newIndicator: Blue dot (8px, accent-500) for unread items
feeds.infiniteScroll: Load next page when 200px from bottom. Show spinner at bottom during load.

skeleton.description: Show skeleton that matches the shape of the content being loaded
rules: Match skeleton shapes to actual content layout
Show immediately — no delay before showing skeleton
Minimum display time: 300ms to prevent flash
Replace skeleton with content using a fade transition (150ms)

spinner.usage: Inline loading for buttons, small areas. Not for full-page loads.
spinner.color: Inherit text color or use interactive-primary

progressBar.usage: Known-duration operations: file uploads, multi-step forms
progressBar.indeterminate: Sliding gradient animation when progress is unknown

empty.description: When a view has no content to display
rules: Always explain why it's empty and what the user can do
Center vertically and horizontally in the available space
Examples: 'No messages yet — Start a conversation', 'No results found — Try a different search'
Never show raw error codes or stack traces to users
Use human language: 'Something went wrong' not 'Error 500'
Log technical details to console/monitoring, not to UI

iconography.system: Use a consistent icon set. Recommended: Lucide, Heroicons, or Phosphor.
iconography.style: Outlined (1.5px stroke) for UI. Filled for active/selected states.

color.rule: Icons always inherit their parent's text color unless they carry semantic meaning (status icons)

iconography.padding: Icons inside buttons/chips: 2px padding from edges. Standalone: 4px clickable padding for touch targets.

accessibility.decorative: aria-hidden='true' — icon is next to text that conveys the same meaning
accessibility.rule: If removing the icon would lose information, it needs an accessible label

placement.buttons: Leading (before label) for actions. Trailing (after label) for navigation (arrows, external links).
placement.inputs: Leading for semantic (search, email). Trailing for actions (clear, toggle visibility).
placement.rule: Never use more than one icon per side of a component

imagery.loading: Show skeleton placeholder with correct aspect ratio. Load image with fade-in (150ms).
imagery.fallback: If image fails to load: show neutral gray background with a subtle image icon.
imagery.objectFit: cover for photos, contain for logos/illustrations
imagery.lazy: All images below the fold use loading='lazy'. Above fold: loading='eager'.

alt.photos: Describe the content: 'Woman using laptop in a cafe'
alt.charts: Describe the trend: 'Bar chart showing revenue growth from $1M to $3M'
alt.rule: Every img must have an alt attribute. No exceptions.

singleLine.css: overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap;

multiLine.usage: Card descriptions (3 lines), list previews (2 lines)

truncation.rule: Always show full content on hover (via tooltip) or via expand action. Never truncate headings.
